title: triticum ovatum var. echinum
intro: Triticum ovatum var. echinum, also known as Einkorn wheat, is a rare and ancient subspecies of wheat that contains two sets of chromosomes, making it a tetraploid species. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Einkorn wheat is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The health benefits of Einkorn wheat are attributed to its high content of antioxidants, fiber, and essential minerals, which may help to promote healthy digestion, boost energy levels, and support overall well-being. Einkorn wheat can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often consumed in its whole grain form as a nutritious and flavorful addition to bread, cereals, and baked goods.
